tool_name: Industrial Roof Structural Load, Area Calculation & Material Cost (ASCE 7 / EN 1991)

inputs.name: Roof Pitch Angle (α)
inputs.hint: Flat roof: 0–5° | Low pitch: 5–15° | Medium: 15–30° | Steep: >30°

inputs.name: Roof Dead Load (self-weight + purlins + services)
inputs.hint: Include cladding + purlins + insulation + MEP hangers — structural engineer to confirm

inputs.name: Roof Live Load (maintenance access)
inputs.hint: ASCE 7: min 0.96 kN/m² (20 psf) | EN 1991-1-1: category H = 0.40 kN/m²

inputs.name: Ground Snow Load (sk)
inputs.hint: From national annex / ASCE 7 Figure 7.2-1 | EN 1991-1-3 map | Turkey TEC 2018

inputs.name: Snow Exposure Factor (Ce)
inputs.hint: ASCE 7 Table 7.3-1: Fully exposed=0.7 | Partially exposed=1.0 | Sheltered=1.2

inputs.name: Snow Thermal Factor (Ct)
inputs.hint: ASCE 7 Table 7.3-2: Heated building=1.0 | Cold storage=1.1 | Unheated=1.2

inputs.name: Importance Factor (Is)
inputs.hint: ASCE 7 Table 1.5-2: Normal occupancy=1.0 | Essential facility=1.1 | Low hazard=0.8

inputs.name: Basic Wind Speed (V)
inputs.hint: 3-second gust at 10m height — from national wind map | EN 1991-1-4 or ASCE 7 Fig. 26.5-1

inputs.name: Terrain / Exposure Category
options: C / III — Open terrain, scattered obstructions (Cp=0.85)
D / IV — Flat open coast, unobstructed (Cp=1.0)

formulas: Gross_Roof_Area_m2 = 2 * Slant_Half_Width_m * building_length_m   // [m²] Total sloped roof area
Overhang_Area_m2 = eave_overhang_m * (2 * building_length_m + 2 * building_width_m)   // [m²] Eave overhang perimeter area
Total_Roof_Area_m2 = Gross_Roof_Area_m2 + Overhang_Area_m2   // [m²] Total area to clad
Order_Area_m2 = Total_Roof_Area_m2 * (1 + waste_allowance_pct / 100)   // [m²] Material order quantity
Material_Cost_USD = Order_Area_m2 * material_cost_usd_m2   // [USD] Roofing material cost
Total_Roof_Cost_USD = Material_Cost_USD + Labour_Cost_USD   // [USD] Total roofing cost
Cost_per_m2_USD = Total_Roof_Cost_USD / Total_Roof_Area_m2   // [USD/m²] All-in unit rate
Roof_Snow_Load_kN_m2 = ground_snow_load_kN_m2 * exposure_factor_Ce * thermal_factor_Ct * importance_factor_Is   // [kN/m²] Design roof snow load — ASCE 7 Eq.7.3-1 / EN 1991-1-3
Dynamic_Wind_Pressure_kN_m2 = 0.5 * 1.25 * POWER(wind_speed_ms, 2) / 1000 * wind_pressure_coeff_Cp   // [kN/m²] Design wind pressure (density=1.25 kg/m³)
Total_Gravity_Load_kN_m2 = dead_load_kN_m2 + live_load_kN_m2 + Roof_Snow_Load_kN_m2   // [kN/m²] Total gravity design load
Total_Snow_Force_kN = Roof_Snow_Load_kN_m2 * building_length_m * building_width_m   // [kN] Total snow load on structure
Uplift_Wind_kN_m2 = -Dynamic_Wind_Pressure_kN_m2 * 0.7   // [kN/m²] Net uplift wind load (suction — approximate)

dimensions_positive.error_msg: Building dimensions must be positive values.

pitch_range.error_msg: Roof pitch must be 0–60°. Angles above 60° are structurally unconventional for industrial buildings.

snow_importance.error_msg: Importance factor must be 0.8–1.2 per ASCE 7 Table 1.5-2.

smart_warnings.message: TOTAL GRAVITY LOAD > 3.0 kN/m²: Heavy combined loading. Structural engineer must design purlins, rafters, and columns specifically for this load. Standard light steel industrial roof frames (Z/C purlins) are typically designed for 0.5–1.5 kN/m². DO NOT use standard span tables.

HIGH SNOW LOAD > 1.5 kN/m²: Significant structural implication. Roof framing must be designed for snow drift accumulation (ASCE 7 Section 7.7) at parapets and higher-level roof adjacencies. Progressive collapse analysis may be required.

FLAT/LOW-PITCH ROOF WITH SNOW: Pitch < 5° prevents snow from sliding. Full unbalanced snow load accumulates. Ponding risk from blocked drainage must also be assessed. Provide minimum 2% fall to drains.

HIGH WIND PRESSURE > 1.2 kN/m²: Cladding fixings and sheet overlaps must be designed for this uplift. Standard trapezoidal sheet fixings rated 0.8–1.0 kN/m² may be insufficient. Verify manufacturer's approved fixing schedule.

Waste allowance < 5% on steeply pitched roof: Hip ends, valleys, and ridge cuts generate significant offcuts. Minimum 8–10% recommended for pitches above 20°. Under-ordering causes installation delays and material price premium on re-order.

WIND UPLIFT EXCEEDS DEAD LOAD: Net uplift force will lift roof structure. Roof-to-wall connections and hold-down anchors are structurally critical elements. Structural engineer must design specific uplift connections — this is not a standard detail.
